action.hypothesis: A JumpReLU encoder with learnable per-latent thresholds can improve the K=128 quality anchor versus plain TopK by suppressing weak activations before top-k selection, improving feature selectivity and lowering FVU without changing the execution layer.
action.summary: Implemented a new `jumprelu` SAE family under `sparsify/` with save/load-compatible config support and a prototype encoder that applies ReLU, a smooth learnable threshold gate, and then top-k selection. This round should run a proxy comparison at the existing K=128 anchor using only the new architecture and its family-specific threshold knobs.
action.self_review: The prototype is coherent and passes local forward/backward plus save/load checks. Main risk is throughput regression because JumpReLU currently uses a dense encode path instead of the fused topk kernel; if proxy quality is promising but runtime degrades, the next step should be a performance follow-up rather than discarding the family.
notes_to_memory: Added a new `jumprelu` SAE family with per-latent learnable thresholds and smooth gating before top-k selection.
JumpReLU config is execution-layer compatible via the existing `ARCHITECTURE`, `JUMPRELU_INIT_THRESHOLD`, and `JUMPRELU_BANDWIDTH` env hooks in the launcher.
Local sanity checks passed for forward/backward and save/load on the new family.
next_hypotheses: If JumpReLU shows better proxy FVU at K=128, tune threshold init and bandwidth before considering any K reduction.
If JumpReLU quality is promising but runtime is flagged as a perf regression, fuse or otherwise optimize its encode path instead of drawing a negative architecture conclusion.
If JumpReLU underperforms badly, try a stronger routing family such as grouped or multi-branch top-k rather than continuing weak local tuning on plain TopK.
